rules_fired.because: Enzymes in 50% glycerol stay liquid at −20°C but denature rapidly above ~4°C. The glycerol stock is viscous, requiring reverse pipetting to eliminate air bubbles. Vortexing creates a high-shear air-water interface that unfolds proteins.
rules_fired.cite: NEB Cloning & PCR guidelines; Eppendorf Technical Guide: Viscous Liquids, 2020.
